title: Thoughts On Stuff Blog Articles
wikitext:
[]: Thoughts On Stuff Blog Articles             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Feb 20, 2011 1:15 pm Check it out. You got nothing better to do today.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Sun Feb 20, 2011 2:54 pm Interesting reads. I object to his notion that Shinji's a "blank slate" but whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Feb 20, 2011 5:33 pm I'll give it a look when I find the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Feb 20, 2011 5:36 pm Another blog? Evageeks is a blog?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Feb 20, 2011 6:33 pm Seems like he only mentions 2.0, and only when listing them under some sort of film list of his. Not really an Evangelion centered blog, but should make for an interesting read.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Feb 20, 2011 6:39 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Seems like he only mentions 2.0, and only when listing them under some sort of film list of his. Not really an Evangelion centered blog, but should make for an interesting read.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Feb 20, 2011 8:38 pm He writes longer reviews than I do...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 20, 2011 8:56 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: He writes longer reviews than I do...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Feb 20, 2011 9:19 pm Sachi wrote: That's because his reviews are less reviews than they are scene-by-scene commentaries.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 20, 2011 9:28 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: From what I skimmed, it's about 80% recap and only 20% commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 20, 2011 11:49 pm The fact that this guy calls the movies " Rebuild of Evangelion X.XX" automatically invalidates all of his opinions.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Feb 21, 2011 6:38 am I actually happened to find and and read most of that a few months ago. I found it pretty interesting. I mean, it didn't really offer anything new and unthinkable that an inveterate fanboy isn't aware of or hasn't heard before, but I did think the guy managed to be fairly insightful at times and apparently independently come to several "correct" conclusions. I liked seeing a non-anime fan's thoughts about Evangelion, and I liked his gushing. Also, at least his rewatch articles for the series and EoE don't involve that much recapping.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Feb 21, 2011 10:05 am Reichu wrote: The fact that this guy calls the movies " Rebuild of Evangelion X.XX" automatically invalidates all of his opinions.

[]: Thoughts On Stuff Blog Article: EoE In-Depth Analysis             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 30, 2011 6:11 pm Merged from here on out with a previous thread. Old article which I was linked to (thanks, Gatotsu !) The content I'm has been discussed to death tons of times before, but this is an interesting read regardless: http://thoughtsonstuff.blogspot.com/2008/08/end-of-evangelion-in-depth.html (The blogger's thoughts on 1.11 are here http://thoughtsonstuff.blogspot.com/2008/09/rewatch-of-rebuild-of-evangelion-10-you.html and 2.22 here: http://thoughtsonstuff.blogspot.com/2010/05/rebuild-of-evangelion-222-you-cannot.html ) Also interesting (thanks again Gatotsu !), which are probably linked elsewhere on the forums: http://www.comicsalliance.com/2011/01/17/alan-moore-hideaki-anno-genre/ http://www.slantmagazine.com/house/2008/08/the-economy-of-visual-language-neon-genesis-evangelion/ (Maybe this belongs more in "Everything Else", I just felt it was fitting for "Discussion". )

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Mar 30, 2011 7:48 pm Just as an addendum, I've exchanged a few emails with Patrick Meaney, author of "Thoughts on Stuff", and Twitteracted several times with Michael Peterson, author of "The Economy of Visual Language". Both are neat-o-riffic fellas with some interesting perspectives on the series and a number of other topics. Peterson in particular wrote what is probably the best review of The Dark Knight I've ever read.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Mar 30, 2011 7:57 pm I only read the first link, but that was absolutely fantastic. This guy pretty much mirrors everything I think about the movie. There was just one thing I kind of disagreed with: She is a fusion of Yui and Lilith, and in the end, she chooses her son over her husband.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed Mar 30, 2011 8:39 pm rhfxz1s wrote: I never saw Rei as a father figure to Shinji at all.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 30, 2011 8:55 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Even though she shares Yui's DNA, I never saw Rei as a father figure to Shinji at all. If anything, she chose Shinji as a friend (or lover, depending on how deep you like reading into things) over Gendo, who really just gave her a life of misery.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Mar 30, 2011 8:58 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Even though she shares Yui's DNA, I never saw Rei as a father figure to Shinji at all. If anything, she chose Shinji as a friend (or lover, depending on how deep you like reading into things) over Gendo, who really just gave her a life of misery.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 30, 2011 9:07 pm By the way, the original link for the three different blog entries can be found here: http://myanimelist.net/blog.php?eid=88004 And about the cast of Eva, the Death and Rebirth Special Edition Film book has this to say (I really should re-sig it): Ayanami Rei -- Bandages. Mysteries. Indifference. An object of interest. Mother. Soryu Asuka Langley -- Girl. Perplexing. Formidable. Indecipherable. Sex. Katsuragi Misato -- Adult. Superior. Meddlesome. Soldier. Family. Ikari Gendo -- Harsh. Commander. Hatred. Anger. Father. Father. Father.... ~Evangelion: Death & Rebirth (Special Edition) Theatrical Program Book
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Sun Feb 20, 2011 2:54 pm Interesting reads. I object to his notion that Shinji's a "blank slate" but whatever.

[]:             Quote Po
